text.title: Backlog Grooming and Agent Integration
text.summary: Five-step grooming process, health targets, agent workflow for automation, and critical rules for human-in-loop control.

section.title: Grooming Process

list.item: Step 1: Capture — accept items from any source, categorize type (feature/bug/debt/research), defer evaluation
Step 2: Groom (weekly, 1-2h) — review new items, refine top items, re-prioritize on new learning, archive stale
Step 3: Prioritize — apply RICE or MoSCoW, re-rank when new information arrives or goals change
Step 4: Refine top-of-backlog — break into smaller stories, add AC, clarify requirements, estimate, identify deps
Step 5: Cleanup — delete duplicates, archive items untouched for 6+ months, merge related items

rule.statement: Archive items untouched for 6+ months AND with no roadmap link. Timestamp alone is not sufficient for stale detection.
rule.rationale: Valid items can be dormant; cross-check with linked customer requests and roadmap before archiving.

section.reference: Health snapshot: counts by status/type, metric table with targets, actions needed.
Session template: new item triage, top-of-backlog review, refinement, estimates, cleanup summary.

rule.statement: Agents draft; humans commit. Never grant write-scoped tokens to autonomous agents on live trackers.
rule.rationale: Agent-applied edits to live tickets in front of users is jarring and error-prone. Route through PR-style changeset or staging branch for human review.

list.item: Triage agent (nightly): classify new items, draft story format, propose priority — outputs draft only
Health-check agent (weekly): compute counts, age distribution, AC coverage %, stale candidates
Groomer agent (synchronous, during session): assists refinement of top-20 items under human approval

antipattern.description: LLM-rewritten user stories that paraphrase the original request.
antipattern.reason: Original requestor wording is signal; agent paraphrasing destroys it. Keep original text under a Raw Request section in every item.
T-shirt sizing without calibration against past delivery is dart-throwing. Bootstrap from last 30 closed items before trusting agent estimates.

antipattern.description: Auto-merging "similar" items based on semantic similarity alone.
antipattern.reason: Wrong merges destroy audit trail and lose distinct customer requests. Require 0.9+ similarity score AND human accept before merging.

section.title: Priority Limit Rule

rule.statement: If more than 20% of the backlog is already P1, refuse to propose new P1 items until the proportion drops.
rule.rationale: P1 inflation means nothing is P1. The label loses its forcing function for sprint ordering.

section.reference: Weekly cron: Linear API pull, health report generation, stale candidate extraction.
